spec_match.rationale: The demo compares the current text-fit values none, grow, shrink, and shrink 60% with a clean unsupported fallback when Chrome 148 lacks the v150 syntax.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the width slider, short/standard/long preset buttons, custom text input, active states, row text, and live measurement readouts all update together.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The index blurb perfectly maps to the demo's purpose of comparing the various text-fit values side-by-side.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo now stress-tests short, standard, long, and custom text so grow, shrink, overflow, and clamp behavior can be compared across fitting and overloaded strings.

references.rationale: The reference list successfully includes the target ChromeStatus entry link.

design_system.rationale: The demo complies with the layout, CSS variable structure, and typography of the global design system.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap: the page had only a width slider and static 'Headline Text'. The repaired page was tested at http://localhost:3000/v150/css-text-fit-property/fit-comparison/?verify=after-text-presets in Chrome 148: Short, Standard, Long, custom text, and 180px/620px slider states updated all four rows, the input value, pressed preset state, status text, and measurement list with no console errors and only successful network requests. The feature conformance route ran at /v150/css-text-fit-property/conformance/?verify=after-fit-comparison-presets with 1 PASS / 6 FAIL / 7 TOTAL, expected because this browser does not yet parse the v150 text-fit syntax; the concept displayed the unsupported fallback cleanly.
